Package coxprogramming.processing.sprites

Class ImageSprite

Object
Sprite
ImageSprite
public class ImageSprite extends Sprite
A sprite that displays an image file instead of a drawn shape.

ImageSprite extends Sprite and works just like any other sprite: it can move, wrap around the screen, follow the mouse, and so on, but instead of drawing a circle or rectangle it draws a picture loaded from a file.

By default the image is drawn 100 by 100 pixels, centered on the sprite's position, with no rotation. Use the size constructor or setSize(float, float) to change its displayed size. Use setRotation(float) or rotate(float) to rotate the image in radians.

Place your image file in your sketch's data/ folder, then: 

   ImageSprite character = new ImageSprite(this, 200, 300, "hero.png");
   ImageSprite largeHero = new ImageSprite(this, 200, 300, "hero.png", 160, 120);

    Constructor
    Description
    ImageSprite(PApplet parent, float x, float y, String fileName)
    Creates an ImageSprite at position (x, y) and loads its image from a file.
    ImageSprite(PApplet parent, float x, float y, String fileName, float size)
    Creates a square ImageSprite with a custom display size.

    Creates an ImageSprite with a custom display width and height.

  • Field Details

    • img

      protected PImage img
      The image displayed by this sprite.

    • w

      protected float h

      protected float rotation

  • Constructor Details

    • ImageSprite

      public ImageSprite(PApplet parent, float x, float y, String fileName)
      Creates an ImageSprite at position (x, y) and loads its image from a file. The file must be in the sketch's data/ folder. The image is drawn 100 by 100 pixels by default.
      Parameters:
      parent - your Processing sketch; pass this
      x - the starting x position (center of the image)
      y - the starting y position (center of the image)
      fileName - the name of the image file, e.g. "hero.png"

    • ImageSprite

      public ImageSprite(PApplet parent, float x, float y, String fileName, float size)
      Creates a square ImageSprite with a custom display size.
      Parameters:
      parent - your Processing sketch; pass this
      x - the starting x position (center of the image)
      y - the starting y position (center of the image)
      fileName - the name of the image file, e.g. "hero.png"
      size - the width and height to draw the image, in pixels

    • ImageSprite

      public ImageSprite(PApplet parent, float x, float y, String fileName, float w, float h)
      Creates an ImageSprite with a custom display width and height.
      Parameters:
      parent - your Processing sketch; pass this
      x - the starting x position (center of the image)
      y - the starting y position (center of the image)
      fileName - the name of the image file, e.g. "hero.png"
      w - the width to draw the image, in pixels
      h - the height to draw the image, in pixels

  • Method Details

    • setSize

      public void setSize(float size)
      Changes this sprite to a square display size.
      Parameters:
      size - the new width and height, in pixels

    • setSize

      public void setSize(float w, float h)
      Changes this sprite's displayed size without changing the source image.
      Parameters:
      w - the new width, in pixels
      h - the new height, in pixels

    • setRotation

      public void setRotation(float rotation)
      Sets this sprite's rotation angle.
      Parameters:
      rotation - the new angle in radians

    • rotate

      public void rotate(float amount)
      Adds to this sprite's current rotation angle.
      Parameters:
      amount - the amount to rotate in radians

    • getRotation

      public float getRotation()
      Returns this sprite's current rotation angle in radians.
      Returns:
      the current rotation angle

    • drawSprite

      public void drawSprite()
      Draws the image centered on this sprite's current position. The image is displayed at this sprite's configured size and rotation. Call this once per frame inside your draw() loop.
      Specified by:
      drawSprite in class Sprite
